betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Melter§f:
The Melter (nuclearcraft:melter) is a single-block fluid-producing machine that converts solid ores, ingots, dusts, gems, and even entire blocks into molten fluids at high temperature.

§6Slot Layout (MelterRecipes.java:18)§f:
1 item input → 0 fluid input → 0 item output → 1 fluid output. The Melter therefore receives one solid stack and emits one fluid stack per cycle.

§6Crafting Recipe (CraftingRecipeHandler.java:85)§f:
Pattern "PNP / NCN / PSP" in a 3×3 grid. Letters: P = plateAdvanced, N = ingotBrickNether, S = servo, C = chassis. Yields 1 Melter.

§6What It Makes (MelterRecipes.java:22-180)§f:
The Melter is the gateway to the entire NC fluid economy. It can melt:
- NC ores and ingots into their fluid forms (e.g. ingotUranium238 → uranium, ingotBoron10 → boron_10, ingotLithium7 → lithium_7, ingotBeryllium → beryllium, ingotHardCarbon → hard_carbon, ingotManganeseDioxide → manganese_dioxide, ingotStrontium90 → strontium_90, ingotMolybdenum → molybdenum, ingotRuthenium106 → ruthenium_106, ingotCesium137 → cesium_137)
- Alloy ingots into alloy fluids (steel, ferroboron, electrical_steel, energetic_alloy, vibrant_alloy, dark_steel, etc.)
- Mod alloys: manasteel, terrasteel, elementium (Botania), psi (Psi), dilithium (Advanced Rocketry), refinedobsidian/refinedglowstone (Mekanism), JAOPCA essence tiers (baseEssence, inferium, prudentium, intermedium, superium, supremium)
- Vanilla: dustSulfur → sulfur, silicon ingot → silicon, blockQuartz → quartz, blockLapis → lapis, blockDiamond → diamond, blockEmerald → emerald, obsidian → obsidian, netherrack/nether_brick → nether_brick, endstone → end_stone, ice/packed_ice → water
- Thermal dusts → fluids: redstone, glowstone, ender, pyrotheum, cryotheum, aerotheum, petrotheum
- Sweets: ground_cocoa_nibs → chocolate_liquor, sugar → sugar, gelatin → gelatin
- ores from various stone types (gravel, netherrack, endstone, sand, blackgranite, redgranite, marble, basalt) when ore_processing is enabled

§6Fission Material Melts (MelterRecipes.java:213-217)§f:
addFissionMeltingRecipes() automatically adds fluid melts for every ingot in NCConfig.FISSION_ORE_DICT mapped to the corresponding FISSION_FLUID entry, ensuring all depleted/recovered fuel forms have a fluid representation.

§6Auto-Generated Ore Melts (MelterRecipes.java:221-239)§f:
addOreMeltingRecipes() iterates every registered fluid in the game. For each fluid where oreDict contains both an ingot and a dust (or gem and dust), it generates the full trio of recipes: ore variants (1.25× multiplier), ingot/dust, nugget/tinyDust, and block forms.

§eTip§f: The Melter is the single most useful NC machine early game — almost every downstream fluid (silicon, boron, lithium, beryllium, steel, ferroboron) is sourced through it. Build a row of Melters with speed upgrades if you plan to scale up fluid production.

§eTip§f: The Melter is implicitly required by many higher-tier NC machines. The Ingot Former casts ingots back from these fluids, the Fluid Mixer/Salt Mixer combines them, the Chemical Reactor consumes them, and QMD's Ore Leacher (a Chemical Reactor descendant) leaches metals from ores using NC fluids.

§cWarning§f: The Melter cannot be turned off without breaking the recipe; treat it as always-on. Some fluid outputs (obsidian, end_stone, nether_brick) require extra processing time and are better batch-produced in a dedicated Melter line.
